Title: Different Functions of IbRAP2.4, a Drought-Responsive AP2/ERF Transcription Factor, in Regulating Root Development Between Arabidopsis and Sweetpotato

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: The study highlights functional divergence of the sweetpotato IbRAP2.4 transcription factor between species: in Arabidopsis overexpression enhances lateral root formation and drought tolerance, whereas in its native sweetpotato background overexpression suppresses storage root formation and increases lignified pencil roots, underscoring species-specific regulatory contexts of AP2/ERF factors.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as a nuclear AP2/ERF transcriptional activator that promotes lateral root formation and enhances drought tolerance when overexpressed in Arabidopsis, but in sweetpotato overexpression inhibits storage root formation and increases lignified pencil roots, associated with activation of lignin biosynthesis genes
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: Nuclear-localized AP2/ERF transcription factor that specifically binds DRE (TACCGACAT) and GCC-box (AGCCGCC) cis-elements and functions as a transcriptional activator, increasing expression of lignin biosynthesis genes (especially COMT and CCR) in sweetpotato roots, which leads to enhanced lignification and altered root type differentiation
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Acts within AP2/ERF-DREB-mediated abiotic stress and hormone-responsive signaling pathways to regulate root development and lignin biosynthesis, by binding DRE and GCC-box cis-elements and activating downstream gene expression
Plant_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Lignin biosynthesis pathway (upregulation of CCR, COMT, CCoAOMT in sweetpotato roots)
Plant_Genes.Upstream_or_Downstream_Regulation: IbRAP2.4 --(transcriptional activation, expression upregulation)--> COMT; IbRAP2.4 --(transcriptional activation, expression upregulation)--> CCR; IbRAP2.4 --(expression upregulation)--> CCoAOMT (all in sweetpotato roots). IbRAP2.4 also activates DRE- and GCC-box-containing reporter gene expression in plant cells.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: AP2/ERF transcription factor, DREB subfamily, containing a typical AP2 DNA-binding domain
Plant_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Nucleus, shown by IbRAP2.4-GFP fusion localized specifically to nuclei in Nicotiana benthamiana leaf cells co-stained with DAPI
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Constitutively expressed in leaves, stems, fibrous roots, pencil roots, and storage roots with higher expression in pigment roots, storage roots, and fibrous roots; induced by NaCl (upregulated peaking at 6 h), slightly and transiently affected by PEG8000-induced dehydration, induced by ethylene (maximum at 24 h), and strongly induced by IAA after 6–15 h in Sushu 16 seedlings
Plant_Genes.Gene_Accession_Number: GenBank CDS: KX255653.1; GenBank protein: ARS72979.1
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: NaCl (150 mM) salt stress, 20% PEG8000 dehydration stress, 100 μM ethylene, 100 μM IAA treatments in sweetpotato seedlings; soil drought (withholding water for 7 days followed by re-watering) in Arabidopsis; field cultivation in Nanjing for sweetpotato root phenotype evaluation
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Root system architecture (lateral and storage root development) and abiotic stress tolerance regulated by an AP2/ERF transcription factor in sweetpotato
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Root system architecture (lateral root formation, storage root and pencil root development); abiotic stress tolerance (drought, salinity, dehydration)
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: In sweetpotato overexpression lines, pencil root number increased to 4.43 (L3)–5.29 (L2) per plant compared with 0.57 in WT; storage root number per plant was notably reduced (from WT 3–5 SRs to fewer in OE lines, qualitatively described). Expression of COMT increased 2.88- to 4.12-fold and CCR 1.53- to 3.43-fold in IbRAP2.4-overexpressing sweetpotato lines compared with WT. Dual-luciferase assays in Arabidopsis protoplasts showed more than a 2-fold increase in luciferase activity from DRE- and GCC-box reporters in the presence of IbRAP2.4. IbRAP2.4 expression is strongly induced by NaCl, with a peak at 6 h.
Plant_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: In Arabidopsis overexpression lines, increases total root production and biomass and enlarges rosette leaves but reduces chlorophyll content; in sweetpotato, overexpression lines show no clear change in drought tolerance but display altered root-type composition with more pencil roots and fewer storage roots
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Sweetpotato cultivars Sushu 16 and Xushu 29; Arabidopsis thaliana ecotype Col-0; transgenic Arabidopsis lines overexpressing IbRAP2.4 (OV1, OV2); transgenic sweetpotato overexpressing IbRAP2.4 (L1–L3, OE1–OE3); tobacco (Nicotiana benthamiana) leaves for transient expression; yeast strain AH109 for transcriptional activation assay; E. coli BL21 (DE3) for GST-IbRAP2.4 protein expression
Plant_Genes.Variety: Sushu 16 (for cloning and expression analysis); Xushu 29 (for overexpression and field phenotype analysis)
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: cDNA cloning and sequence analysis; domain and phylogenetic analysis; promoter cis-element prediction; qRT-PCR for tissue-specific, stress-, and hormone-responsive expression and for lignin-pathway gene expression; generation of CaMV35S:IbRAP2.4 overexpression plants via Agrobacterium-mediated transformation; phenotyping of root architecture and storage/pencil root numbers in field-grown sweetpotato; drought treatment assays in Arabidopsis; EMSA using GST-IbRAP2.4 and DRE/GCC-box probes; dual-luciferase reporter assays in Arabidopsis protoplasts; yeast transcriptional activation assay in strain AH109; subcellular localization of IbRAP2.4-GFP in N. benthamiana leaves by confocal microscopy; chlorophyll content measurement with SPAD meter
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Generation of CaMV35S:IbRAP2.4 overexpression lines in Arabidopsis thaliana (Col-0) and sweetpotato (Xushu 29) with phenotypic analysis; EMSA showing specific binding to DRE and GCC-box elements; dual-luciferase reporter assays in Arabidopsis protoplasts demonstrating transcriptional activation; yeast transcriptional activation assay; subcellular localization of IbRAP2.4-GFP fusion by confocal microscopy
Plant_Genes.Field_Trials: Untransformed and IbRAP2.4-overexpressing sweetpotato plants (Xushu 29 background) were transplanted into the field in Nanjing, Jiangsu, and grown to maturity to evaluate storage root and pencil root numbers and morphology.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Predicted from the Sushu 16 sweetpotato transcriptome database and cloned from Sushu 16 cDNA using gene-specific primers; promoter region obtained from sweetpotato genome database
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: IbRAP2.4 is proposed as a molecular target for sweetpotato improvement: functional analyses show that it strongly influences root system architecture by promoting lateral root formation (in heterologous Arabidopsis) and shifting sweetpotato roots from storage roots to more lignified pencil roots through activation of lignin biosynthetic genes. Understanding and modulating IbRAP2.4 activity could aid molecular breeding strategies aimed at optimizing storage root development, root architecture, and stress responsiveness for high-yield sweetpotato, for example by fine-tuning its expression or activity to balance lignification and storage root swelling.
